comments: Bad IV, rat was going crazy and not going under, and the tail looked darker than normal, so I aborted the ketamine infusion.

Clozapine was given at ~minute 15. Clozapine was in a 0.01 mg/mL dilution, using 1% DMSO (made by first mixing 1 mg clozapine with 1 mL DMSO). No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine concentration = 100 mg/mL. Dilution = 0.6 mL ketamine + 19.4 mL saline. This guy also had pink urine.

Clozapine was given at ~minute 15. Clozapine was in a 0.01 mg/mL dilution, using 1% DMSO (made by first mixing 1 mg clozapine with 1 mL DMSO). No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine concentration = 100 mg/mL. Dilution = 0.6 mL ketamine + 19.4 mL saline. Pump stalled with about 30 s to go, so I pushed in (very) roughly 0.5 mL more.
Bad IV, rat was going crazy and not going under, and the tail looked darker than normal, so I aborted the ketamine infusion.

Clozapine was given at ~minute 10. Clozapine was in a 0.01 mg/mL dilution, using 1% DMSO (made by first mixing 1 mg clozapine with 1 mL DMSO). No clave; used luer-lock cap. Dex concentration = 100 ug/mL. Dilution = 0.5 mL dex + 20 mL saline.

CNO was given at 11:32 am, ~1 hr before the start of the ketamine infusion. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Only 10 ml of ketamine (rather than 11.9). Edited the timestamp in line 23 of the events file based on the video because it was clearly wrong.
Saline was given at 11:06 am, ~1 hr before the start of the ketamine infusion. No clave; used luer-lock cap. Ketamine dilution was made by putting 3 mL of 100 mg/mL ketamine in 47 mL saline.
